  • The stock has seen a 2.4% decline over the last 7 days and a 10.7% decline over the last 30 days, while returns are 8.3% year to date and 21.1% over the past year.

Approach 1: Koninklijke Vopak Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For Koninklijke Vopak, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flows in €. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €540.2 million. Analyst estimates are available up to 2028, with forecast free cash flow of €274.35 million in that year. Simply Wall St extrapolates further figures out to 2035 using its own growth assumptions.

Each of these projected cash flows, such as the discounted values of €119.73 million for 2026 and €255.18 million for 2035, is discounted back and summed to reach an estimated fair value per share. This process produces an intrinsic value of €87.65 per share.

Compared with a share price of about €41.32, the model output implies the stock is 52.9% undervalued based purely on these cash flow assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Koninklijke Vopak Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Koninklijke Vopak, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much you are paying for each €1 of current earnings. It links directly to what matters most for equity holders, which is the earnings available to them today.

What counts as a "normal" or "fair" P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk usually supports a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower multiple.

Koninklijke Vopak currently trades on a P/E of 8.04x. That sits below the Oil and Gas industry average of 15.63x and also below the peer group average of 29.81x. data.title: Revolution Medicines Adds RM-055 And First Line Data To RAS Story
data.description:
• Revolution Medicines (NasdaqGS:RVMD) reported preclinical data for RM-055, a new class of catalytic RAS(ON) inhibitors that convert oncogenic RAS to an inactive state in multiple cancer models.
  • The company also shared updated first line clinical data for daraxonrasib in untreated metastatic pancreatic cancer, supporting further Phase 3 evaluation.
  • These announcements expand beyond prior late stage updates that focused on second line pancreatic cancer data and related financings.

For investors tracking Revolution Medicines at a share price of $135.3, the stock has had a sharp move over the past year, with a 249.0% return and a very large gain over 3 years. Year to date, the stock is up 71.2%, although it has seen a 9.0% decline over the past week, which may reflect investors reassessing near term expectations after a 43.5% gain over 30 days.

These RM-055 preclinical results and first line daraxonrasib data provide additional context for how broadly the RAS focused pipeline could reach across tumor settings and treatment lines. As Phase 3 enrollment advances and the RM-055 program matures, upcoming readouts and trial design updates are likely to influence how the market views NasdaqGS:RVMD’s longer term opportunity set in oncology.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Revolution Medicines by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Revolution Medicines.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Revolution Medicines following this latest news.

For existing and potential shareholders, the RM-055 and first line daraxonrasib updates add another layer to an already crowded RAS story. RM-055 sits earlier in the pipeline, but the idea of a catalytic inhibitor that can convert oncogenic RAS back to its inactive state and address resistance could matter for how long current and future RAS(ON) drugs stay relevant in the treatment sequence. At the same time, fresh first line daraxonrasib data in untreated metastatic pancreatic cancer connects directly to the ongoing RASolute 303 trial and builds on the previously treated RASolute 302 results, which already met all primary and key secondary endpoints. Together, this gives investors more clinical detail to weigh against a share price that has moved sharply, and a capital structure that now includes a US$1.5b equity raise and US$500m of convertible notes. The combination of maturing late stage assets, earlier stage pipeline optionality and recent financings helps explain why trading has been active as different investors reassess risk, reward and time horizon.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Revolution Medicines Narrative

  • The new RM-055 resistance focused data and first line daraxonrasib experience both align with the narrative that a multi drug RAS(ON) portfolio can address high unmet need tumors across several lines of therapy, potentially extending treatment duration per patient.
  • The same concentration in RAS targeted programs highlighted in the narrative also means that setbacks in RM-055 or first line pancreatic trials would affect more than one part of the story at once, which could challenge expectations built around multiple successful Phase 3 programs.
  • The specific concept of a catalytic RAS(ON) inhibitor aimed at resistance, along with how investors react to the large recent equity and convertible raises, may not be fully captured in existing community narratives that focus more on RASolute 302 and headline expense guidance.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Revolution Medicines to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Revolution Medicines currently reports no revenue and is guiding for US$1.6b to US$1.7b of GAAP operating expenses in 2026, so the company is relying on trial success and future approvals to justify high cash use.
  • ⚠️ All core assets target the RAS pathway, so competition from other RAS and immuno oncology drugs from players such as Amgen, Bristol Myers Squibb and AstraZeneca, as well as potential resistance mechanisms, could limit long run market share.
  • 🎁 RASolute 302 showed a large overall survival benefit versus chemotherapy in metastatic pancreatic cancer, supported by FDA Breakthrough Therapy and Orphan Drug designations, which could support a differentiated position if approved.
  • 🎁 Zoldonrasib’s early lung cancer data with a 52% objective response rate and a 93% disease control rate in KRAS G12D NSCLC, together with RM-055’s preclinical resistance story, suggest a pipeline that extends beyond a single tumor setting and gives multiple shots on goal within RAS driven cancers.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors will want to follow how detailed first line daraxonrasib data in metastatic pancreatic cancer feed into the RASolute 303 program, including safety, durability of response and any subgroup outcomes that could matter for future labeling and uptake. For RM-055, the key questions are how quickly the program moves toward human studies and whether early clinical signals eventually confirm its preclinical resistance profile. It is also worth watching how the market digests the recent equity and convertible offerings over time, including any updates on cash runway, additional Phase 3 commitments and potential partnerships with larger oncology players.

data.title: Aichi (TSE:6345) Margin Improvement Supports Steady Earnings Narrative Despite Modest Growth
data.description:
Aichi (TSE:6345) has wrapped up FY 2026 with fourth quarter revenue of ¥21,459.4 million and basic EPS of ¥48.50, backed by trailing twelve month revenue of ¥59,613 million and EPS of ¥100.73. Over the past few quarters the company has seen revenue move from ¥17,930.8 million in Q4 FY 2025 to ¥21,459.4 million in Q4 FY 2026, while quarterly EPS shifted from ¥34.43 to ¥48.50. This points to modest earnings momentum, and an 11.2% trailing net margin gives investors a results season focused on profitability resilience rather than breakneck growth.

See our full analysis for Aichi.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the key narratives investors follow around Aichi's growth, quality and income appeal.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

TTM EPS Tops ¥100 With Steady Margin

  • Over the last twelve months, Aichi earned basic EPS of ¥100.73 on revenue of ¥59,613 million, with net income of ¥6,658 million and a trailing net margin of 11.2%, compared with 10.7% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is how this steady profile lines up with the business being an equipment supplier to essential infrastructure work. The margin improvement from 10.7% to 11.2% and earnings growth of 5.1% over the past year both fit with the idea of a durable, maintenance driven revenue base, even though revenue growth of 2.6% per year remains relatively modest compared with the broader JP market at 5.9% annual growth.
    • Supporters of a bullish angle may point to five year earnings growth of 2.1% per year alongside the recent 5.1% increase as evidence that profitability has held up over a reasonably long stretch of time.
    • At the same time, the slower 2.6% revenue growth rate versus the JP market means any bullish argument is built more on earnings quality and margin consistency than on fast top line expansion.

For a closer look at how these profit trends fit into the longer term story around growth, risks, and valuation, it helps to see how different investors are interpreting the same set of numbers through their own narratives Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives.

P/E Of 13.1x Sits Below Machinery Peers

  • Aichi trades on a trailing P/E of 13.1x, which is below both the JP Machinery industry average of 14.6x and a peer average of 17x, while the current share price of ¥1,346 is also about 0.7% below the stated DCF fair value of ¥1,356.04.
  • Supporters of a bullish stance could argue that this combination of a lower P/E and a small discount to DCF fair value is consistent with the idea of a relatively conservative price tag on steady earnings, yet the modest 2.6% annual revenue growth and 2.1% five year earnings growth rate also limit how strong that case can be.
    • On one hand, the 11.2% net margin and five year earnings growth do heavily support the view that Aichi has been able to sustain profitability, which can make a below peer P/E look reasonable to bullish investors.
    • On the other hand, the slower revenue growth versus the broader JP market shows why some investors might see the lower P/E as more of a reflection of moderate growth expectations than a clear cut bargain.

4.46% Dividend Faces Cash Flow Question Mark

  • The trailing dividend yield sits at 4.46%, and the key flag in the data is that this payout is not comfortably covered by free cash flow over the same twelve month period.
  • Critics taking a more bearish angle often focus on income sustainability, and the combination of a 4.46% yield with tight free cash flow coverage directly feeds that concern, especially when revenue growth is 2.6% annually and earnings growth over five years is 2.1% per year, which means there is not a lot of rapid growth in the background to ease pressure on the payout.
    • This tension between a relatively high yield and weaker free cash flow coverage can challenge any bearish claim that the company is purely a low risk income name, because the numbers highlight that the payout relies on cash generation that has been tight.
    • At the same time, the presence of an 11.2% net margin and recent 5.1% earnings growth shows that profitability at the income statement level remains sound, which is an important counterpoint when thinking about how serious that dividend coverage issue may be.

data.description:
HKBN (SEHK:1310) has just posted its H1 2026 scorecard, with trailing 12 month revenue of HK$11.4b and basic EPS of HK$0.14, setting the tone for how the year is shaping up after a period of faster earnings growth.   • On a trailing 12 month basis, HKBN earned HK$207.0 million of net income on HK$11.4b of revenue, which works out to a 1.8% net margin compared with 1.1% a year earlier and sits behind the 78% earnings growth over the same period.
  • Analysts' consensus view links that margin progress to premium services such as higher speed broadband and bundled ICT solutions. However, the current 1.8% net margin and modest forecast revenue growth of about 1.5% a year leave less room for error if those bundles or value added services do not gain as much traction as expected.
    • Consensus narrative points to GigaFast broadband, CyberZafe and Infinite play bundles as ways to lift average revenue per user, but the recent net income of HK$207.0 million on HK$11.4b of sales underlines how tightly profitability is still tied to small changes in margins.
    • The same narrative expects revenue to reach around HK$11.8b in coming years, which is close to the current HK$11.4b trailing level, so a lot of the earnings story relies on the assumed increase in profit margin rather than large top line expansion.

High 58.5x P/E against HK$6.50 target and HK$20.15 DCF fair value

  • The shares trade on a trailing P/E of 58.5x at a price of HK$8.19, compared with an analyst price target of HK$6.50 and a separate DCF fair value estimate of about HK$20.15, so the current price sits between a lower analyst target and a higher cash flow based value.
  • What stands out for the bullish narrative is that analysts see earnings reaching HK$849.3 million and EPS of HK$0.57 by around 2029. Yet the same framework assumes the P/E would compress to 14.3x on those earnings, which is well below the current 58.5x even though it is similar to the 14.4x cited for the wider HK Telecom industry.
    • Bulls often focus on the gap between HK$8.19 and the HK$20.15 DCF fair value, but the HK$6.50 analyst target shows that not all models point in the same direction using the same earnings base.
    • The requirement for profit margins to move from about 1.9% to 7.2% alongside earnings rising from roughly HK$206.9 million to HK$849.3 million illustrates how much of the optimistic case is tied to substantial margin expansion on relatively steady revenue of roughly HK$11.8b.
On these numbers, bulls are effectively betting that margin expansion and higher earnings will arrive fast enough to make today's multiple look tame, while the DCF and analyst target point in very different directions on where that could leave the share price over time. 🐂 HKBN Bull Case

  • Interest payments are flagged as not well covered by earnings and the 4.2% dividend yield is described as not well covered either, which sits awkwardly with a trailing 1.8% net margin and net income of about HK$207.0 million on HK$11.4b of revenue.
  • Bears highlight that earnings have declined by an average of 14.8% per year over the past five years, and combine that with weak interest cover and the uncovered dividend to question whether the recent 78% one year earnings lift and forecast 38.7% annual earnings growth can support both debt servicing and a cash payout if revenue only grows around 1.5% a year.
    • The increase in cash through financing activities and focus on deleveraging in the narrative tie back directly to the flagged major risk around interest coverage, which depends on maintaining or improving that 1.8% margin.
    • With earnings over five years trending down on average despite the latest year on year jump, the cautious view is that leverage and dividend commitments could restrict flexibility if margin gains or ICT related upsell do not materialise as expected.

Approach 1: Tapestry Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

The Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to a present value using a required rate of return.

For Tapestry, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections rather than earnings multiples. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $1.65b. Analyst estimates and further extrapolated figures indicate projected Free Cash Flow of $2.11b in 2035, with intermediate years such as 2028 forecast at $1.69b. Simply Wall St applies discounts to each of these annual cash flows. As a result, the projected $2.11b in 2035 equates to a discounted figure of $872.20m in today’s terms.

Combining all discounted cash flows gives an estimated intrinsic value of US$131.33 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$145.89, the DCF output suggests the stock is about 11.1% overvalued based on these assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Approach 2: Tapestry Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much investors are paying for each dollar of earnings. It links the share price directly to the company’s profits, which is often what ultimately drives long term returns.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower P/E.

Tapestry currently trades on a P/E of 56.6x. This sits well above the Luxury industry average P/E of 19.6x and also above the peer average of 24.9x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Tapestry is 28.6x, which is its own estimate of what P/E might be reasonable given factors like earnings growth, industry, profit margin, market cap and risks.

The Fair Ratio can be more useful than just comparing with peers or the broad industry because it adjusts for Tapestry’s specific growth profile, risk characteristics and profitability.   • Recent coverage has focused on Ethos Technologies as part of a broader look at insurance names, highlighting how sentiment can shift quickly when investors reassess risk and growth potential. This backdrop helps explain why the share price has been so active in the short term.
  • Right now the company scores 2 out of 6 on our valuation checks. The rest of this article will walk through the main valuation approaches investors often use, before finishing with a way to tie those numbers to a fuller story about the stock.

Ethos Technologies scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Ethos Technologies Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much value a company can create over and above the return that equity investors typically require. Instead of focusing on cash flows, it starts with earnings and book value, then asks whether those earnings represent a premium over the cost of equity.

For Ethos Technologies, the model uses a stable earnings figure of about $0.92 per share, based on the median return on equity over the past 5 years. Against a cost of equity of $0.39 per share, this implies an excess return of roughly $0.53 per share. The average return on equity used in the model is 16.37%, applied to a stable book value of $5.59 per share, which comes from weighted future book value estimates from 2 analysts.

Feeding these inputs into the Excess Returns framework gives an estimated intrinsic value of around $20.32 per share. Compared with the current price of about $16.77, this suggests the shares trade at roughly a 17.5% discount within this model.

Result: Potential undervaluation according to this model

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests Ethos Technologies is undervalued by 17.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 56 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Ethos Technologies.

Approach 2: Ethos Technologies Price vs Earnings

For a company that is generating earnings, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to see how much investors are paying for each dollar of profit. It is popular because it links directly to earnings per share, which is a key input for many valuation models.

What counts as a fair P/E usually reflects two things: the market’s expectations for future earnings growth, and how risky those earnings are perceived to be. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk typically aligns with a lower multiple.

Ethos Technologies currently trades on a P/E of about 14.8x. This is above the Insurance industry average of roughly 11.6x, but below the peer group average of around 34.9x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what P/E might be reasonable given factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics. This tends to be more tailored than a simple comparison with industry or peer averages, because it adjusts for company specific traits rather than assuming all insurers deserve the same multiple.

As the Fair Ratio is not available for Ethos Technologies here, it is not possible to formally classify the shares as overvalued or undervalued using this framework.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT
